title: Oracle layoffs: Employees in India, US, & other regions receive email at 6am
content: Oracle has started laying off employees across its global offices, with workers receiving termination emails at 6AM from "Oracle Leadership" informing them their roles had been eliminated. Teams like RHS, SVOS, and NetSuite IDC were among the first hit. The job cuts could hit 20,000 to 30,000 people—roughly 18% of Oracle's workforce—as the company looks to free up cash for its AI data centre push.

title: 'Trump doesn't bluff': Pentagon chief warns Iran, says US wants deal but 'prepared to bomb'
content: Pentagon chief Pete Hegseth declared upcoming days "decisive" in the Middle East conflict, warning Iran to "cut a deal" as President Trump "doesn’t bluff."  He emphasized shifting power dynamics and US options, urging Tehran to relinquish ambitions and reopen the Strait of Hormuz.  Escalation continues with recent attacks impacting oil prices.

title: Murder convict MP BJP corporator Kallu Kushwaha shot dead in Datia
content: A BJP corporator, Kallu Kushwaha, was shot dead in broad daylight in Datia, sparking panic and raising law and order concerns. The victim, who had a criminal past and recently served a life sentence, was attacked by unidentified assailants. Police are investigating personal enmity and have rounded up suspects, examining CCTV footage.

title: Starting April 1, USCIS alert says that the immigration authority will reject forms
content: USCIS will only accept the 02/27/26 edition of Form I-129 for H-1B and other visa applications starting April 1, 2026. The updated form requires employers to detail educational requirements and experience, aligning with the Department of Labor's wage level assignments for the new wage-weighted lottery system.

title: 'No plan B, C or D': FIFA asserts Iran will play World Cup in US
content: "Iran will be at the World Cup... That's why we're here. We're delighted because they're a very, very strong team, I'm very happy," Infantino said at half-time during Iran’s friendly against Costa Rica in Turkey.

title: Bihar maulana arrested, apologises for remarks targeting UP CM Yogi Adityanath’s mother
content: A Bihar cleric, Maulana Abdullah Salim, has publicly apologized after being arrested by Uttar Pradesh STF for allegedly making objectionable remarks against UP CM Yogi Adityanath's mother. Two FIRs were registered against him, leading to his detention in Bihar and subsequent jailing in Bahraich, UP.

title: UK King Charles to tour US from April 27-30th for 1st time as Monarch
content: In a monumental move, King Charles III and Queen Camilla are gearing up for a state visit to the United States this April, a trip that coincides with the nation's 250th anniversary of independence. Amid rising tensions related to the Iran conflict and prevailing political discord, the royal couple aims to fortify diplomatic relations.

title: What Sam Altman said is the goal of OpenAI, Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei complains we are close to reaching that but there is no…
content: OpenAI's Sam Altman claims AGI is within reach, with AI agents set to transform workplaces this year. Rival Anthropic's CEO, Dario Amodei, echoes the rapid progress but expresses deep concern over society's lack of preparedness. He warns of a looming 'tsunami' of AI advancement, with governments and the public failing to grasp the profound implications and risks.

title: Sexual violence used as weapon of war in Sudan, aid group says
content: Doctors Without Borders reported treating over 3,300 sexual violence survivors in Sudan between January 2024 and November 2025, highlighting rape as a weapon of war. Many survivors faced significant delays in receiving critical care, with armed men identified as perpetrators in most cases. The aid group urged the UN to increase its presence to address the widespread crisis.

title: 'Let him be at peace': Himanta attacks Cong over Zubeen's mention in manifesto
content: Assam CM Himanta Biswa Sarma accused the Congress of "disgusting politics" for including singer Zubeen Garg’s death in their manifesto. He stated the party committed an "unpardonable crime" by using the late singer's name for votes, asserting justice is already being pursued in a fast-track court.

title: 'Chennai offered double of BCCI's prize': Culture that made CSK a champion
content: Chennai Super Kings' enduring IPL success stems from consistent ownership and player retention. Stephen Fleming's long tenure as coach has fostered a winning culture. A unique tradition sees the franchise double BCCI prize money for players, showcasing their commitment to welfare. This approach has built a stable, experienced team.

title: IPL: Priyansh Arya not in XI as Punjab Kings bowl first against Gujarat Titans
content: For the Punjab Kings, Priyansh Arya did not make it to the starting XI. However, the star batted was named in the impact subs list and is expected to come to bat in the second innings.

title: Court gives time to Priya Kapur in Rs 30k inheritance row with Mandhira Kapur Smith
content: A legal battle over a Rs 30,000 crore inheritance is intensifying. Following businessman Sunjay Kapur's death, his wife Priya has filed a defamation case against his sister, Mandhira. The court granted Priya time to respond to Mandhira's request for specific documents related to the ongoing defamation proceedings.

title: End of Red Terror? How close India is to its Naxal-free target as March 31 deadline ends
content: India stands on the cusp of eradicating Naxalism, with Union Home Minister Amit Shah declaring the movement effectively dismantled.  Decades of development and security operations have significantly shrunk the Red Corridor, pushing the insurgency into isolated pockets.  While armed rebellion may be over, securing lasting peace through governance remains crucial.

title: Stray bullet kills ex-brigadier: How Dehradun nightclub bill dispute spiraled into road shootout
content: A retired Brigadier, Mukesh Kumar Joshi, was tragically killed by a stray bullet during an exchange of fire between occupants of two SUVs. The incident, initially thought to be road rage, stemmed from a billing dispute at a nightclub the previous night. Police have arrested four individuals and are pursuing others involved in the deadly confrontation.

title: Was Trump destined to plunge world into war?
content: The notion lives on in a post-colonial society where English – and who has access to it – is a marker of civility and civilisation. The Higgins-Macaulay Complex continues to haunt post-colonial societies, but there are times when the English language can be oddly prescient.

title: Pakistan's Fakhar Zaman banned for ball-tampering in PSL
content: "Match Referee Roshan Mahanama conducted the disciplinary hearing and made the determination after reviewing all evidence," the statement added.

title: Quote of the day: ‘You can’t climb ladder of success with hands in pockets’
content: Arnold Schwarzenegger's journey from Austrian immigrant to global icon exemplifies his mantra: "You can't climb the ladder of success with your hands in your pockets." His relentless drive, evident from bodybuilding championships to Hollywood stardom and governorship, underscores a life built on active participation and unwavering ambition.

title: BCCI issues statement on death of IPL 2026 broadcast engineer
content: Langford, 76, was found unconscious in his hotel room in south Mumbai on Monday morning and was taken to Bombay Hospital, where he was declared dead. Police said no suspicious findings were reported in the postmortem, and a case of unnatural death has been registered.

title: Ex-Army captain on the run after murdering wife caught after LPG booking reveals his hideout
content: Convicted former army captain Sandeep Tomar, on the run for four years after being sentenced for strangulating his wife, has been arrested in Madhya Pradesh. His capture followed a coordinated effort by Fazilka police and NATGRID, who traced him through digital footprints, including financial transactions and an LPG refill. Tomar was apprehended in Pandhurna district, ending a high-profile pursuit.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO Day 13: Ranveer Singh-starrer eyes Rs 900 crore net milestone
content: 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' continues its record-breaking run on day 13, surpassing the lifetime India net of 'Dhurandhar Part 1' in just 11 days. With a domestic net of Rs. 873.99 crore and a worldwide gross nearing Rs. 1,400 crore, the Ranveer Singh starrer is successfully defying the IPL 2026 season craze. Directed by Aditya Dhar, the film is now chasing the Rs. 900 crore and eventually the Rs. 1000 crore India net milestone, a feat previously seen only by the Allu Arjun starrer 'Pushpa 2.'

title: BJP releases Kerala manifesto: Rs 3,000 pension, AIIMS and big Sabarimala promise
content: The BJP's Kerala election manifesto pledges a Rs 3,000 monthly welfare pension for needy women and seniors, alongside 20,000 litres of free water per household. The party also promised two free LPG cylinders annually for poor families and a Rs 2,500 monthly health card. Key development plans include an AIIMS and a high-speed rail network.

title: Candace Owens questions Charlie Kirk murder probe over 'no evidence' claim
content: Candace Owens has reignited debate over the killing of Charlie Kirk after reports claimed the bullet recovered from his body did not match Tyler Robinson’s rifle. She criticized those who supported the case against Robinson, calling the evidence “non existent.” While investigators cite alleged confessions, the new forensic detail has raised fresh questions and intensified public scrutiny around the trial.

title: IndiGo appoints former British Airways chief William Walsh as new CEO
content: IndiGo Airlines has appointed former British Airways chief William Walsh as its new CEO, effective August 3, 2026. This strategic move follows the resignation of Pieter Elbers earlier this month. Walsh, currently with IATA, brings extensive global aviation experience and a customer-focused leadership style to guide IndiGo's next phase of growth and transformation.

title: ‘I’ll quit when I’m bored’: Shami on retirement despite repeated India snubs
content: Veteran pacer Mohammed Shami has stated retirement is not on his mind, emphasising he will only step away when he feels bored, not due to age, selection, or injuries. Despite being out of the national setup for over a year, Shami remains focused on his performance and enjoying the game, aiming to deliver for his new team, Lucknow Super Giants.

title: I-T changes from April 1: Top 10 things salaried taxpayers should know about new rules
content: Several changes are expected to reduce the tax burden for the salaried class in general. We take a look at top 10 things salaried taxpayers should know going into the new financial year 2026-27:

title: Bengaluru techie jumps to death from 17th floor after finding partner dead in locked room
content: A techie couple from Telangana died tragically within minutes of each other in Bengaluru's Kothanur area. The husband was found hanging in a locked room, and his wife subsequently jumped from the 17th floor of their apartment building. Police are investigating multiple angles, including health, relationship issues, and emotional distress.

title: 27-year-old Indian-origin man shot dead in Canada’s Surrey
content: A 27-year-old man of Indian origin, Jasman Sekhon, was fatally shot in Surrey, Canada, in what authorities suspect is a targeted attack linked to organized crime and the drug trade. The brazen incident occurred Saturday evening, with witnesses reporting a chase before the shooting.

title: Brittany Mahomes reacts to Randi Mahomes’ emotional message on Patrick
content: Brittany Mahomes’ induction into the University of Texas at Tyler Athletics Hall of Fame highlights her own athletic journey, separate from her public life today. A former collegiate standout who briefly played professionally in Iceland, she remains connected to her roots. Her heartfelt speech and the support from Randi Mahomes reflect both personal pride and a continued commitment to uplifting women’s sports.

title: IPL 2026 tragedy: British broadcast engineer found dead in Mumbai hotel
content: A 76-year-old British broadcast engineer, Jan William Langford, working for the IPL 2026 in Mumbai, was found dead in his hotel room. Hotel staff discovered him unconscious and he was declared dead on arrival at the hospital. Police have registered a case of accidental death after a postmortem revealed no suspicious findings.

title: Ready to win big -- or be a big-time disappointment? The 5 most polarizing MLB teams
content: Is your team a threat to win big -- or a disappointment waiting to happen? It depends on who is asked.

title: Tiger said he was looking at phone before crash
content: Tiger Woods told authorities that he was looking down at his phone and changing the radio station before his rollover crash last week in Florida, according to a probable cause affidavit.

title: Cuban: I regret selling Mavs to Adelson, Dumont
content: Mark Cuban says he regrets selling the Mavericks to the Adelson and Dumont families, adding that he "made a lot of mistakes in the process."

title: Sources: M's, Emerson agree to historic contract
content: Mariners shortstop Colt Emerson, ESPN's No. 6 prospect for 2026 who has yet to play in the majors, has agreed to an 8-year, $95M extension, sources confirmed to ESPN.

title: Rioux, Florida's 7-foot-9 center, enters the portal
content: Olivia Rioux, the Florida Gators' 7-foot-9 center and the tallest player in NCAA in college basketball history, has entered the transfer portal.

title: Yanks go 5-for-5 on ABS challenges; Boone irked
content: The Yankees won all five of their challenges utilizing the automated ball-strike system during Monday night's game against the Mariners.

title: Bulls waive guard Ivey after anti-gay comments
content: The Chicago Bulls waived guard Jaden Ivey after his recent anti-gay comments amid several rants on religion and other topics.

title: 'Nature calls': Edwards misses tipoff in return
content: Timberwolves star Anthony Edwards missed the start of Monday night's game in his return from a six-game absence because he had to go to the bathroom.

title: Wemby posts 3rd-fastest double-double since '97
content: Victor Wembanyama had a season-high 41 points and 16 rebounds while collecting the third-fastest double-double since 1997-98 in the Spurs' win Monday.

title: A.P. Health Minister Satya Kumar Yadav flags declining fertility rate, calls for immediate corrective measures
content: At 1.50, the Total Fertility Rate is below the replacement rate of 2.10, and further decline will result in ageing population and decline in productive workforce, he says

title: Kerala Assembly poll 2026: FCRA debate reshapes political narrative in Central Travancore
content: What was, until recently, an aggressive outreach has now been overshadowed by a surge of concern over the Union government’s proposed amendments to the Foreign Contribution Regulation Act. The perception that the law can be weaponised to tighten control over minority institutions has begun to dominate discourse, particularly in central Travancore

title: Defeat Congress candidate in Davangere, Ashok tells Muslims
content: The Leader of Opposition says Samarth Mallikarjun’s victory in the bypolls will deny Muslims Congress ticket for the next 50 years

title: T.N. Assembly election 2026: Poll promises delivered and undelivered during 5 years of DMK government
content: The govt. introduced schemes that did not figure in its manifesto, even while delivering key assurances such as Kalaignar Magalir Urimai Thittam and free bus travel; but it has not formulated the Higher Education policy, set up Kalaignar Canteens or created special courts for corruption cases

content: “If Mr. Modi says jump, your Chief Minister will jump. If he says lie down, the Chief Minister will lie down. If he says give this to [Gautam] Adani, he will do it”

title: Tamil Nadu CM Stalin, TVK leader Vijay and other leaders file nominations for 2026 Assembly election on day one
content: The DMK president and Tamil Nadu Chief Minister submits his papers to the Returning Officer for the Kolathur constituency. TVK president C. Joseph Vijay, who is making his debut in electoral politics, files his nomination for Perambur

title: Sanand stands as a ‘bridge’ to Silicon Valley: PM on Gujarat town’s rise in semiconductor ecosystem
content: While inaugurating a semiconductor assembly and test facility of Kaynes Semicon in Sanand, he said there was rapid expansion in the country’s electronics sector and that India’s semiconductor market could exceed $100 billion by the end of the decade

title: Disappointed over confusion post bifurcation, electors say with little development and strained civic infrastructure, Tirukkoyilur is totally neglected
content: Tirukkoyilur Assembly Constituency is witnessing a generational shift with sitting MLA and DMK strongman K. Ponmudi having been been denied seat and replaced by his son and former MP Gowtham Sigamani. For now, the DMK treads a tricky path of battling anti-incumbency while the AIADMK hopes to cash in on it

title: School students develop modular storm-water filtration system to address pollution of waterbodies
content: It addresses the need for a simple, low-cost, and easily deployable filtration solution to reduce pollutants in stormwater before it enters the city’s lakes and other waterbodies

title: Public meetings, lectures to mark Ambedkar Jayanti programme
content: The Kalaburagi district unit of the Ambedkar Jayanti Utsav Samiti will organise the three-day programme from April 12

title: Anti-war music festival in Thiruvananthapuram
content: The festival titled ‘K\ala/pam - Music Concert for Peace’ will be organised by the collective ‘Humans of World’

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026: AIIMS, high-speed rail network on BJP manifesto
content: NDA releases its ‘Vikasita Keralam Vision’ manifesto that promises a radical job revolution and a “politics of performance” to transform the State’s economic and social landscape

title: The Hindu, SRMIST to host sessions on careers in public policy, international relations
content: The event, ‘The Future Reimagined: Discover, Decide, Define Your Career’, is part of the Future Career Conversations series organised by the SRM Institute of Science and Technology (SRMIST) and The Hindu, and will be held at the Madras Management Association, Anna Salai, between 5.30 p.m. and 7.30 p.m.

title: At least one village in every district to be developed as model solar village, says Minister for Energy
content: Minister Ravi Kumar directed officials to ensure timely completion of solar projects while maintaining standards; he also called for increasing awareness among consumers on the use of solar panels

title: 2026 T.N. Assembly election is a battle between secularism and polarisation: Thamizhachi Thangapandian
content: Thamizhachi Thangapandian says that the DMK is very comfortable and confident of facing the poll; she also says that the DMK does not consider the TVK as a serious contender and appeals to youngsters to be more politically conscious

content: The electoral battle in Kerala is shaping up as a direct confrontation between the ruling CPI(M)-led LDF and an invigorated Congress-led UDF, with the BJP-led NDA struggling to muscle in as a third force in the sweepstakes

content: Supply of petrol and diesel exceeds demand by 2.4% and 8% respectively, says Manohar, adding that data is being monitored twice daily to assess the situation

content: He says despite Kerala’s immense human potential, the State witnessed negligible industrial growth under successive LDF and UDF governments, thereby leading to economic stagnation and fiscal stress

title: Nalanda University’s revival symbol of commitment to re-establish its glory in modern form: President Murmu
content: President Murmu said the Nalanda university had established itself as a great centre of knowledge for about eight centuries, and its fall was a very big loss not only for India but for the entire world

title: Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu to tour Nellore and Kadapa districts on April 1
content: First he will disburse NTR Bharosa pensions among the beneficiaries at Vinjamur in Nellore district and later participate in the Kalyanotsavam at the Kodandarama Swamy temple at Ontimitta in Kadapa district

title: Trump taunts world saying go get oil from inflamed Iran — or buy from America
content: incendiary social media posts on tuesday, he told allies to "go get your own oil" from an inflamed Gulf region or buy american energy . he taunted allies, implicitly accusing them of cowardice . at the heart of the growing rift is a perception across european capitals that the US launched the war without consultation .

title: 2026 rankings: Profiling the top 35 QBs
content: the 2024 NFL MVP has finished in the top 10 in passing TDs six seasons in a row and remains a major threat with his legs . despite seeing less passing volume the past two seasons (14th or lower in pass attempts both years), he has finished second among quarterbacks in fantasy points . his 21.4 fantasy points per game in 2025 was actually his lowest since 2019 .

title: Sabalenka excels, Sinner back on track and more takeaways from Indian Wells and Miami Open
content: the "sunshine double" and tennis' version of March Madness are elusive feats . just four women and seven men had achieved back-to-back victory in singles play . despite everything mentioned above, the Sunshine Double was achieved not just once over the weekend .

title: Previewing the McDonald's All American boys' game: Stokes vs. Branch, more to watch
content: the McDonald's All American boys' game annually brings together the nation's top prospects in the senior high school class . all 24 players in this year's game are ranked inside the ESPN 100 -- including the top seven players in the class, headlined by no. 1 Tyran Stokes and no. 2 Jordan Smith .

title: Heavyweight madness: Which big fella would win a 16-man tournament?
content: the men's and women's basketball tournaments haven't stopped us from creating our own hypothetical tournaments for the past few years . the big fellas will take center stage in this year's bracket . former uFC and PFL heavyweight champ Alex Pereira will move up to MMA's premier weight class on may 14 .

title: MMA divisional rankings: Grasso, Pyfer elevate their standing in knockout performances
content: the main event pitted two-time former middleweight champion Israel Adesanya against rising Joe Pyfer . he scored a second-round knockout for his fourth straight victory . former women's flyweight champion Alexa Grasso finished fellow top-10 fighter Maycee Barber in the first round .

title: World Cup European playoffs, live updates: Italy looking to make their return
content: The final spots for the 2026  FIFA World Cup  will be decided Tuesday. In Europe, four matches will determine which teams will be coming to this summer's tournament in North America. Here are the UEFA matches that will be played Tuesday with the winners earning a spot:  Bosnia and Herzegovina  vs.  Italy ,  Sweden  vs.  Poland ,  Kosovo  vs.  Türkiye  and  Czechia  vs.  Denmark . Enjoy all the live updates from Tuesday's matches!

title: Top newcomers for Notre Dame and every Big Ten team; what coaches, GMs are saying
content: coach Curt Cignetti says he ripped Michigan State transfer Nick Marsh for wearing gold cleats to practice . no conference in 2025 better felt how quality newcomers can reshape rosters like the Big Ten .

title: Perfect team fits for 20 NFL draft prospects
content: the 20 best players-to-team matches for rounds 1 and 2 are speculative . a lot of head coaching changes from this offseason will lead to plenty of system changes . but the idea was to find spots where players would be put in position to succeed because of their traits and the existing infrastructure .

title: Early Men's Final Four preview: Arizona-Michigan, UConn-Illinois predictions
content: the 2026 men's final four is set for saturday's national semifinals against Illinois . the final weekend of the tournament is the final of the NCAA tournament . for the season, Arizona ranked fifth in the country in paint points, averaging 42 .

title: What's next for Duke, Tennessee after Elite Eight: Recruits, transfer portal, more
content: the 2026 men's NCAA tournament has featured its fair share of heartbreaking losses . storied programs didn't reach the regionals such as Kansas and North Carolina . ESPN's Jeff Borzello and Myron Medcalf outline expected departures and returnees .
